issues.display_title: Issue #1669: [Slicing] Build Promo Delete Confirmation Modal
issues.main_description_html:
Type: Slicing (frontend build) · Epic: Delete promo (/promocodes) · ref #1662

Build the delete confirmation modal (design: deletepromocodes.png).

Scope: .confirm-dialog — trash icon, "Delete this promo code?", "'' will be deleted immediately. This can't be undone.", Cancel / Delete (danger). role=dialog, aria-modal, focus trap, Esc/scrim close, focus returns to trigger.

Acceptance: confirm-by-name; accessible; opens from a list row's Delete action.

Part of the Support Console redesign (Clients + Promo Codes CRUD).
Refs: docs/15-06-2026/plan.md · docs/15-06-2026/brainstorm.md. Related: #1658–#1662 (Project 32).

Demo by Developer

QA Tester Summary - Issue #1669: [Slicing] Build Promo Delete Confirmation Modal

issues.display_title: Issue #1679: Bug: Duration Radio Buttons Clickable in Readonly/ Disabled State (promo code form)
issues.main_description_html:
Problem

On the promo code form, when a promo code is opened in read-only / disabled mode (edit mode), the Duration options (One time / Forever / Limited period) can still be clicked. Clicking them makes all options appear selected at the same time, which is incorrect.

Expected behavior

  • In read-only / disabled mode: the Duration options cannot be clicked and their appearance does not change.
  • In add mode: the Duration options work normally, with only one option selected at a time.

Demo by Developer

2026-06-22.17-10-39.mp4

QA Tester Summary

Suite / Environment / Overall Result

  • Suite: Issue #1679 - Promo Code Duration Radio Disabled-State Validation
  • Environment: https://dev.villume.com/promocodes/edit/TITSRCCM/cHJvbW9fMVRsNUdDRXE5dm9XSk1haUtVTVJzSXds and https://dev.villume.com/promocodes/add
  • Support account: villumesupport@gmail.com
  • Test data: existing promo TITSRCCM / test cup 14
  • Build verification: Passed with npm.cmd run build:dev:ssr
  • Overall Result: Passed

Test Scenario & Result

Scenario Result Notes
Load existing promo in edit/read-only mode Passed Promo TITSRCCM loaded with Forever selected as the current duration.
Verify edit-mode duration disabled state before clicks Passed One time, Forever, and Limited period rendered as disabled radio inputs.
Click all duration options in edit/read-only mode Passed Selection did not change after clicking all duration labels; only Forever remained selected.
Verify no multiple selected duration state in edit mode Passed DOM checked state and PrimeNG visual checked class both stayed single-selected.
Verify add-mode duration behavior Passed Add mode allowed switching One time -> Forever -> Limited period -> One time, with one selected option at a time.
Build verification Passed npm.cmd run build:dev:ssr completed successfully.

Bug Notes

  • The bug described in the issue was not reproduced on the current dev build.
  • Actual retest result:
    • Edit/read-only mode: duration radio buttons are disabled and do not change when clicked.
    • Edit/read-only mode: no multiple-selected visual state was observed.
    • Add mode: duration radio buttons remain interactive and single-select as expected.
  • Expected behavior is now met.

Code Review

Area File / Component Result Notes
Edit mode disable handling promocode-form.component.ts Passed _disableAllInput(['name']) disables the form in edit mode while keeping only the promo name editable.
Duration change guard promocode-form.component.ts Passed changeDuration() returns early when the target duration control is disabled.
Duration radio binding promocode-form.component.html Passed Each p-radioButton receives [disabled] from its matching duration form control.
Duration visual state promocode-form.component.scss Passed Retest confirmed the checked styling remains aligned with the selected disabled value.

Response Code Review

Request Result Notes
GET /v1/support/products HTTP 200 Product metadata loaded for promo form options.
GET /v1/support/coupons/detail/{promo_id}?stamp=0 HTTP 200 Existing promo detail loaded for edit/read-only validation.
Duration click attempts in edit mode No mutation request Clicking disabled duration options did not trigger an update request or state change.

White / Gray / Black Box Coverage

Test Type Coverage Result
White box Reviewed edit-mode disable flow, duration radio disabled bindings, and changeDuration() disabled guard. Completed
Gray box Captured DOM checked/disabled state, PrimeNG visual checked classes, and related API response codes. Completed
Black box Clicked duration options in edit/read-only mode and add mode exactly as a support user would. Completed

Confusion Matrix

Area Passed Failed Skipped
Edit form load 1 0 0
Edit-mode disabled duration behavior 3 0 0
Add-mode duration behavior 1 0 0
API/build verification 2 0 0

Evidence

  • Screenshots and video attached below show the edit-mode disabled duration state before clicks, after click attempts, and add-mode single-select behavior.

Final QA Decision

Passed. The reported disabled-state duration radio bug is no longer reproducible on dev. Edit/read-only mode stays locked, and add mode still works normally.

Screenshots

Add mode duration radio buttons remain single-select:

Edit/read-only mode after clicking all disabled duration options:

Edit/read-only mode duration state before click attempts:

Video Recording

Full validation recording for edit-mode disabled duration and add-mode single-select behavior:

issue-1679-duration-radio-validation.mp4

issues.display_title: Issue #1668: [Integration] Wire Promo List Search, Filter & Pagination
issues.main_description_html:
Type: Integration (wiring) · Epic: Promo Codes list (/promocodes) · ref #1659

Wire the list to the existing server-side endpoint.

Scope: getPromoCodes(status, page, limit) with is_active for all/active/expired; honor the refetch-promocode localStorage flag on return from the form.

Acceptance: server search/filter/pagination intact; list refreshes after add/edit/delete.

Part of the Support Console redesign (Clients + Promo Codes CRUD).
Refs: docs/15-06-2026/plan.md · docs/15-06-2026/brainstorm.md. Related: #1658–#1662 (Project 32).

Demo by Developer

2026-06-18.14-58-29.mp4

QA Tester Summary

Suite / Environment / Overall Result

  • Suite: Issue #1668 - Promo Codes List Search, Filter & Pagination Integration
  • Environment: https://dev.villume.com/promocodes
  • Support account: villumesupport@gmail.com
  • Search scope: Skipped per dev confirmation.
  • Build verification: Passed with npm.cmd run build:dev:ssr
  • Overall Result: Needs Retest / Bug Note

Test Scenario & Result

Scenario Result Notes
Load promo list from server endpoint Passed /promocodes rendered 10 rows with total count and pagination range from the server-backed list.
Active filter Passed Clicking Active requested GET /v1/billing/coupons?is_active=true&page=1&limit=10&stamp=1 and visible rows were active.
Expired/inactive filter Passed Clicking Expired requested GET /v1/billing/coupons?is_active=false&page=1&limit=10&stamp=2; no active rows were displayed.
Pagination Passed Next page requested GET /v1/billing/coupons?page=2&limit=10&stamp=1 and UI changed to Showing 11-20.
Server-side search Skipped Dev confirmed to skip search validation for this issue.
List refresh after add Needs Retest Dedicated #1668 retest could not be completed because dev login/root/add route rendering was unstable and timed out.
List refresh after edit Needs Retest Dedicated #1668 retest could not be completed because dev login/root/add route rendering was unstable and timed out.
List refresh after delete Needs Retest Dedicated #1668 retest could not be completed because dev login/root/add route rendering was unstable and timed out.
Build verification Passed npm.cmd run build:dev:ssr completed successfully.

Bug Notes

  • No blocking bug was found in the tested list/filter/pagination scope.
  • Dev confirmed search can be skipped.
  • Bug Note / Needs Retest: Dedicated add/edit/delete refresh retest for this issue was attempted, but dev login/root/add route rendering repeatedly timed out and made the website appear to keep loading. This flow is not stable enough for final QA approval and should be retested after the dev environment is stable.

Code Review

Area File / Component Result Notes
List endpoint promocodes.service.ts Passed getPromoCodes(refetch, status, page, limit) calls v1/billing/coupons with page, limit, and optional is_active.
Active/expired filters promocode-list.component.ts Passed filterByStatus() resets page to 1, updates route query params, and calls _getPromoCodes(true).
Pagination promocode-list.component.ts Passed changePage() updates query params and calls _getPromoCodes(true).
Add/edit return refresh promocode-form.component.ts Passed Successful form save calls redirectToPromoCodeList(), which sets localStorage.refetch-promocode = true.
List init refetch flag promocode-list.component.ts Passed ngOnInit() reads and removes refetch-promocode, then passes the value into _getPromoCodes(refetch).
Delete refresh promocode-list.component.ts Passed Delete success calls _getPromoCodes(true) after closing the modal.
Search promocode-list.component.ts Skipped Search is currently client-side on loaded rows; dev confirmed search validation can be skipped.

Response Code Review

Request Result Notes
GET /v1/billing/coupons?is_active=true&page=1&limit=10&stamp=1 HTTP 200 Active filter server request captured.
GET /v1/billing/coupons?is_active=false&page=1&limit=10&stamp=2 HTTP 200 Expired/inactive filter server request captured.
GET /v1/billing/coupons?page=2&limit=10&stamp=1 HTTP 200 Pagination server request captured.
POST /v1/billing/coupons Previously verified Linked add flow returned HTTP 200 and refreshed the list.
PUT /v1/billing/coupons/{code} Previously verified Linked edit flow returned HTTP 200 and refreshed the list.
DELETE /v1/support/coupons/{code} Code path verified Delete success refreshes the list with refetch=true.

White / Gray / Black Box Coverage

Test Type Coverage Result
White box Reviewed service query construction, filter/page handlers, localStorage refetch flag, and delete refresh logic. Completed
Gray box Captured network requests and HTTP 200 responses for active filter, expired/inactive filter, and pagination. Completed
Black box Used the /promocodes UI to switch filters and paginate, then verified row state and pagination text. Completed

Confusion Matrix

Area Passed Failed Skipped / Limited
Server list load 1 0 0
Filter integration 2 0 0
Pagination integration 1 0 0
Search 0 0 1
Add/edit/delete refresh 0 0 3
Build verification 1 0 0

Evidence

  • Screenshots attached below show the server-backed promo list, active filter, expired/inactive filter, and pagination page 2.

Final QA Decision

Needs Retest / Bug Note. The tested server filter and pagination wiring works correctly, and search was skipped per dev confirmation. However, the dedicated add/edit/delete refresh flow could not be completed because the dev login/root/add route repeatedly timed out, so the issue should remain open for retest.

Screenshots

Active filter requests is_active=true and shows active promo rows:

Expired/inactive filter requests is_active=false and shows non-active promo rows:

issues.display_title: Issue #1674: [Integration] Wire Edit Promo Code (Name-Only Save)
issues.main_description_html:
Type: Integration (wiring) · Epic: Edit promo (/promocodes/edit/:code/:promo_id) · ref #1661

Wire edit mode to the existing API.

Scope: prefill via getPromoCode(promo_id) + _setPromoCodeFormValues; save via editPromoCode({ name, code, promo_id }); returns to list with refresh.

Acceptance: loads existing values; name update persists; non-name fields are not sent.

Part of the Support Console redesign (Clients + Promo Codes CRUD).
Refs: docs/15-06-2026/plan.md · docs/15-06-2026/brainstorm.md. Related: #1658–#1662 (Project 32).

Demo by Developer

2026-06-18.15-12-21.mp4

QA Tester Summary

Suite / Environment / Overall Result

  • Suite: Issue #1674 - Edit Promo Code Name-Only Integration
  • Environment: https://dev.villume.com/promocodes/edit/:code/:promo_id
  • Support account: villumesupport@gmail.com
  • Test data: automation-created promo GIZDSYJ5
  • Cleanup: automation-created promo deleted successfully after test
  • Build verification: Passed with npm.cmd run build:dev:ssr
  • Overall Result: Failed

Test Scenario & Result

Scenario Result Notes
Create automation-owned promo for edit test Passed Created QA1674 511620 so existing production-like data was not modified.
Load edit form with existing values Passed Edit page loaded created promo values via detail API.
Submit name-only edit Passed PUT /v1/billing/coupons/GIZDSYJ5 returned HTTP 200.
Verify edit payload contract Passed Request payload only sent name, code, and promo_id; non-name fields were not sent.
Return to list with refresh Passed App returned to /promocodes; list showed updated name QA1674 E511620.
Reload edit detail after update Failed Detail reload still returned cached/stale data instead of the edited name.
Cleanup automation-created promo Passed DELETE /v1/support/coupons/GIZDSYJ5 returned HTTP 200.
Build verification Passed npm.cmd run build:dev:ssr completed successfully.

Bug Notes

  • Bug: Edit detail reload can show stale cached data after a successful name update.
  • Actual:
    • Edit request succeeded with HTTP 200.
    • List refresh showed the edited name QA1674 E511620.
    • Reopening/reloading the edit route requested GET /v1/support/coupons/detail/{promo_id}?stamp=0, and the response was marked cached/stale, so the form did not reliably show the updated name.
  • Expected:
    • After a successful edit, the detail route should refetch or invalidate cached coupon detail so getPromoCode(promo_id) prefill shows the updated name.
  • Impact:
    • Support can see the updated name in the list, but reopening the edit screen may show old detail data, causing confusion and risking follow-up edits from stale state.

Code Review

Area File / Component Result Notes
Edit payload builder promocode-form-helper.service.ts Passed createEditPromoCodePayload returns only name, code, and promo_id.
Edit save service promocodes.service.ts Passed editPromoCode sends PUT /v1/billing/coupons/{code} with the edit payload.
Return to list promocode-form.component.ts Passed Successful edit redirects to /promocodes and sets refetch-promocode.
Detail prefill promocode-form.component.ts / promocodes.service.ts Failed Detail reload still uses cached detail (stamp=0) after edit, so the form can show stale values.

Response Code Review

Request Result Notes
POST /v1/billing/coupons HTTP 200 Created automation-owned QA promo for the edit test.
GET /v1/support/coupons/detail/{promo_id} HTTP 200 Loaded initial edit form values.
PUT /v1/billing/coupons/GIZDSYJ5 HTTP 200 Name update succeeded.
GET /v1/billing/coupons?page=1&limit=10&stamp=1 HTTP 200 List refresh reflected the edited name.
GET /v1/support/coupons/detail/{promo_id}?stamp=0 after edit Failed behavior Detail response was cached/stale after update.
DELETE /v1/support/coupons/GIZDSYJ5 HTTP 200 Automation-created promo cleanup succeeded.

White / Gray / Black Box Coverage

Test Type Coverage Result
White box Reviewed edit payload builder, edit service route, and redirect/refetch logic. Completed
Gray box Verified network method, route, response codes, and payload keys. Completed
Black box Created a QA promo, edited only its name, verified list refresh, reloaded detail, and cleaned up test data. Completed

Confusion Matrix

Area Passed Failed Skipped
Create/setup 1 0 0
Edit prefill 1 0 0
Edit payload/update 2 0 0
List refresh/persistence 1 0 0
Detail reload after update 0 1 0
Cleanup/build 2 0 0

Evidence

  • Screenshots attached below show the automation-created promo, edit prefill, name-only save, updated list, stale detail reload, and cleanup.

Final QA Decision

Failed / Bug. The name-only edit request and list refresh work, but the edit detail route can reload stale cached data after a successful update.

Screenshots

Cleanup delete confirmation for automation-created promo:

Automation-created promo visible in list before edit:

Automation-created promo ready for edit integration test:

Edit form prefilled from detail API:

Bug evidence: detail reload after edit can show stale cached data:

Edited promo name visible in refreshed list:

Name-only edit before submit:

issues.display_title: Issue #1673: [Slicing] Redesign Edit Promo Code Form (Read-Only Except Name)
issues.main_description_html:
Type: Slicing (frontend build) · Epic: Edit promo (/promocodes/edit/:code/:promo_id) · ref #1661

Reuse the Add form layout for edit mode (title "Edit promo code").

Decision (brainstorm §1b): all fields read-only except Promo code name — matches _disableAllInput(['name']); the backend only updates the name. Present the full config read-only so support can see it.

Acceptance: only Name is editable; other fields visibly read-only; layout matches Add.

Part of the Support Console redesign (Clients + Promo Codes CRUD).
Refs: docs/15-06-2026/plan.md · docs/15-06-2026/brainstorm.md. Related: #1658–#1662 (Project 32).

Demo by Developer

2026-06-18.15-12-21.mp4

QA Tester Summary

Suite / Environment / Overall Result

  • Suite: Issue #1673 - Edit Promo Code Form Slicing
  • Environment: https://dev.villume.com/promocodes/edit/TITSRCCM/cHJvbW9fMVRsNUdDRXE5dm9XSk1haUtVTVJzSXds
  • Support account: villumesupport@gmail.com
  • Build verification: Passed with npm.cmd run build:dev:ssr
  • Overall Result: Failed

Test Scenario & Result

Scenario Result Notes
Load edit promo form Passed Page loads with Edit promo code title, Add form layout, and live preview.
Verify only promo code name is editable Passed Promo code name remains enabled and editable.
Verify other fields are read-only/disabled Passed Code, product, billing, discount, duration, expiration, and max redemptions are read-only/disabled.
Verify full config is visible read-only Failed Restrict to a user section is hidden in edit mode, so full promo config is not visible.
Mobile layout check Passed Mobile viewport has no horizontal scroll.
Build verification Passed npm.cmd run build:dev:ssr completed successfully.

Bug Notes

  • Bug: Edit mode hides the Restrict to a user section.
  • Actual: Edit mode displays Details, Product & discount, and Duration & limits, but the Restrict to a user section is not visible.
  • Expected: Acceptance says the full config should be presented read-only so support can see it. The restricted user configuration should be visible in edit mode, read-only/disabled like the other non-name fields.
  • Impact: support cannot inspect whether the promo is restricted to a specific user from the edit form.

Code Review

Area File / Component Result Notes
Edit title promocode-form.component.html Passed userAction === 'edit' renders Edit promo code.
Shared Add layout promocode-form.component.html Passed Edit mode reuses the same form card structure and live preview as Add.
Name editability promocode-form.component.ts Passed _disableAllInput(['name']) leaves only name enabled after details load.
Non-name fields promocode-form.component.ts / template Passed Product, billing, discount, duration, expiration, and max redemption controls are disabled/read-only.
Restricted user visibility promocode-form.component.html Failed The Restrict to a user section is wrapped in @if (userAction === 'add'), so edit mode cannot display the restricted-user config.

Response Code Review

Check Result Notes
Detail fetch Passed Existing edit route loaded promo detail data into the form.
Save/update request Skipped This is a slicing/read-only acceptance test; no edit request was submitted.
Build Passed npm.cmd run build:dev:ssr completed successfully.

White / Gray / Black Box Coverage

Test Type Coverage Result
White box Reviewed edit mode template guard, form disable behavior, and preview component usage. Completed
Gray box Verified form control enabled/disabled state and rendered promo detail state. Completed
Black box Tested /promocodes/edit/:code/:promo_id layout, read-only behavior, missing restricted user section, and mobile layout. Completed

Confusion Matrix

Area Passed Failed Skipped
Layout / title 1 0 0
Editable/read-only controls 2 0 0
Full config visibility 0 1 0
Mobile / build 2 0 0
Save request 0 0 1

Evidence

  • Screenshots attached below show the edit form layout, editable name field, read-only fields, missing restricted-user section, and mobile layout.

Final QA Decision

Failed / Bug. Edit mode mostly matches the slicing requirement, but it does not present the full config because the restricted-user section is hidden.

issues.display_title: Issue #1671: [Slicing] Redesign Add Promo Code Form + Live Preview
issues.main_description_html:
Type: Slicing (frontend build) · Epic: Add promo (/promocodes/add) · ref #1660

Rebuild promocode-form.component (add mode) markup (design: PromoCodeEdit.html / addpromocodes.png).

Scope: .form-card sections (Details / Product & discount / Duration & limits / Restrict to a user) + .form-summary live preview.

Decision (brainstorm §1): real API fields only — discount type Percentage / Amount (no Free trial), billing Monthly / Yearly / Both (no Lifetime), duration once/forever/repeating. Drop the custom Code + Generate input (code is server-generated → show it read-only after save).

Acceptance: preview mirrors inputs; name rule (≤20, alphanumeric+space); no design-only inputs that don't persist.

Part of the Support Console redesign (Clients + Promo Codes CRUD).
Refs: docs/15-06-2026/plan.md · docs/15-06-2026/brainstorm.md. Related: #1658–#1662 (Project 32).

Demo by Developer

2026-06-18.15-07-21.mp4

QA Tester Summary

Suite / Environment / Overall Result

  • Suite: Issue #1671 - Add Promo Code Form Slicing + Live Preview
  • Environment: https://dev.villume.com/promocodes/add
  • Support account: villumesupport@gmail.com
  • Build verification: Passed with npm.cmd run build:dev:ssr
  • Overall Result: Failed

Test Scenario & Result

Scenario Result Notes
Load /promocodes/add add form layout Passed Required sections render: Details, Product & discount, Duration & limits, Restrict to a user, and Live preview.
Verify real API field options only Passed Discount options are Percentage and Amount; billing options are Monthly, Yearly, and Monthly and Yearly; no Free trial or Lifetime billing option appears.
Verify live preview mirrors percentage input Passed Product, discount, duration, max uses, and restriction preview update from the form input.
Verify live preview mirrors amount input Passed Switching discount type to Amount updates the input affix and preview to dollar amount.
Verify live preview mirrors repeating duration Passed Yearly billing plus Limited period 2 mirrors as 2 years in the preview.
Validate promo name rule Passed Max 20 characters and alphanumeric plus space validation messages are shown correctly.
Confirm no custom/design-only Code input remains in add mode Failed Add mode still renders a disabled/read-only Code field with placeholder ------.
Confirm add mode CTA copy Failed Submit button still says Save changes, which reads like edit mode.
Confirm no obsolete Lifetime wording remains Failed Promo name placeholder still says e.g. Lifetime Pro Coupon.
Verify optional label punctuation Passed Optional labels render cleanly in the browser.
Responsive layout check Passed Desktop, tablet, and mobile checks showed no horizontal scroll; preview stacks below form on narrower viewports.
Build verification Passed npm.cmd run build:dev:ssr completed successfully.

Bug Notes

  • Bug 1: Add mode still shows a Code field before save.

    • Actual: Code is visible in add mode as a disabled/read-only field with placeholder ------.
    • Expected: the task says to drop the custom Code + Generate input. Since the code is server-generated, it should only be shown read-only after save if needed.

  • Bug 2: Add mode submit button uses edit-mode copy.

    • Actual: submit CTA says Save changes.
    • Expected: add mode should use add/create wording, for example Add promo code or Create promo code.

  • Bug 3: Promo name placeholder still references Lifetime.

    • Actual: placeholder says e.g. Lifetime Pro Coupon.
    • Expected: no Lifetime wording should appear because the scope removes Lifetime as a supported concept.

Code Review

Area File / Component Result Notes
Add form sections promocode-form.component.html Passed Required .form-card sections render: Details, Product & discount, Duration & limits, and Restrict to a user.
Live preview PromocodePreviewComponent Passed Preview mirrors discount, product, duration, expiration, max uses, and restricted user.
Code field in add mode promocode-form.component.html Failed Add mode still renders #p-code in the Details section even though the issue asks to drop the custom Code + Generate input.
Submit CTA copy promocode-form.component.html Failed Add mode and edit mode currently share the submit label Save changes.
Placeholder wording promocode-form.component.html Failed Name input placeholder still contains obsolete Lifetime wording.

Response Code Review

Check Result Notes
Create request Skipped This is a slicing/frontend task, so no create request was submitted.
Product/support user API loading Not blocking API loading was not treated as a blocker for #1671 because the issue scope is slicing and live preview behavior.
Build Passed npm.cmd run build:dev:ssr completed successfully.

White / Gray / Black Box Coverage

Test Type Coverage Result
White box Reviewed add form markup, preview component, route action, and helper validation rules. Completed
Gray box Verified live preview state against selected form values and validation state. Completed
Black box Tested /promocodes/add for layout, options, preview behavior, validation, CTA copy, and responsive behavior. Completed

Confusion Matrix

Area Passed Failed Skipped
Layout / sections 1 0 0
Field options 1 0 0
Live preview 3 0 0
Validation 1 0 0
Copy / obsolete fields 0 3 0
Responsive / build 2 0 0

Evidence

  • Screenshots attached below show the initial layout, field options, live preview updates, validation, responsive checks, and the three bug points.

Final QA Decision

Failed / Bug. The main add form layout, real field options, live preview behavior, validation, responsive layout, and build are working, but three add-mode slicing details still violate the issue scope.

issues.display_title: Issue #1672: [Integration] Wire Add Promo Code Form To API
issues.main_description_html:
Type: Integration (wiring) · Epic: Add promo (/promocodes/add) · ref #1660

Wire the add form to the existing promo API.

Scope: API-driven product types (getProductSupport), allowed-user picker (getListUsers, single email), build the addPromoCode payload (percent_off/amount_off, duration/duration_in_months, expires_on, max_redemptions, metadata.applicable_products, allowed_user). Server generates the code.

Acceptance: create persists; returns to list with refresh; validation + error toasts intact.

Part of the Support Console redesign (Clients + Promo Codes CRUD).
Refs: docs/15-06-2026/plan.md · docs/15-06-2026/brainstorm.md. Related: #1658–#1662 (Project 32).

Demo by Developer

2026-06-18.15-09-07.mp4

QA Tester Summary

Suite / Environment / Overall Result

  • Suite: Issue #1672 - Add Promo Code API Integration
  • Environment: https://dev.villume.com/promocodes/add
  • Support account: villumesupport@gmail.com
  • Build verification: Passed with npm.cmd run build:dev:ssr
  • Overall Result: Failed

Test Scenario & Result

Scenario Result Notes
Load /promocodes/add with API-driven product types and allowed-user picker Passed Form loaded and called GET /v1/support/products and GET /v1/support/users/email with HTTP 200 responses.
Validate product type, billing period, discount type, duration, redemption limit, and generated-code placeholder Passed Required controls were present and usable before submit.
Block SQL-style input in promo name Passed x OR 1=1 -- was blocked by UI validation before submit. No create request was sent.
Block XSS-style input in promo name Passed <script>alert(1)</script> was blocked by UI validation before submit. No create request was sent.
Search allowed user by testergothru@gmail.com Failed API returned HTTP 200, but the picker showed No results found, so the user could not be selected.
Submit a valid promo payload Passed POST /v1/billing/coupons returned HTTP 200 and created a server-generated promo code.
Return to promo list and refresh results after create Passed The created QA promo appeared on the promo code list after submit.
Cleanup created QA promo Passed Created QA promo was deleted through DELETE /v1/support/coupons/{code} with HTTP 200.

Bug Notes

  • Allowed-user API/search data is not correct or not usable for the picker.
  • Request tested: GET https://app2-dev.villume.com/v1/support/users/email?search=testergothru@gmail.com
  • Actual result: HTTP 200 response, but the UI picker displayed No results found.
  • Expected result: testergothru@gmail.com should appear as a selectable allowed user when the API/search response is valid for that email.
  • Impact: the add promo flow cannot create a promo restricted to the requested allowed user. The create payload falls back to "allowed_user": null.

Code Review

  • promocodes.service.ts is wired to the expected dev API routes:
    • GET /v1/support/products
    • GET /v1/support/users/email
    • POST /v1/billing/coupons
  • promocode-form.component.ts builds the create payload with:
    • percent_off / amount_off
    • duration / duration_in_months
    • expires_on
    • max_redemptions
    • metadata.applicable_products
    • allowed_user
  • UI validation blocks special-character promo names before submit.

Response Code Review

  • Product support API: HTTP 200
  • Allowed-user initial API: HTTP 200
  • Allowed-user search API for testergothru@gmail.com: HTTP 200, but no selectable result in UI
  • Create promo API: HTTP 200
  • Promo list refresh API: HTTP 200
  • Cleanup delete API: HTTP 200

White / Gray / Black Box Coverage

  • White box: reviewed route/service/form payload wiring against the issue scope.
  • Gray box: verified network requests, request payload, response status codes, and list refresh behavior.
  • Black box: tested the support UI from login through add promo, validation, allowed-user search, create success, list visibility, and cleanup.
  • Security coverage: SQL-style and XSS-style name inputs were blocked by UI validation before any create request was sent.

Confusion Matrix

Area Passed Failed Skipped
API wiring 4 1 0
UI validation 2 0 0
Create/list refresh 2 0 0
Cleanup 1 0 0

Evidence

  • Screenshots attached below show the add form, validation blocking, allowed-user picker failure, successful create/list refresh, search result, and cleanup.
  • Video attached below shows the browser address bar and the full tested flow.

Final QA Decision

Failed / Bug. The main create flow works, persists, returns to the list, and cleans up correctly, but the allowed-user picker cannot select testergothru@gmail.com because the API/search data is not usable in the UI picker.
